description: An ocean energy researcher at Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is evaluating the feasibility of deploying a pilot tidal turbine array in Admiralty Inlet, Puget Sound, WA. Model a 10-device tidal stream turbine array using PySAM's MhkTidal and Lcoefcr modules to calculate annual energy production, capacity factor, and levelized cost of energy (LCOE). The tidal resource (omnidirectional velocity-probability distribution) consists of velocities (m/s) [0.0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 4.5, 5.0] and probabilities [0.050, 0.120, 0.180, 0.220, 0.180, 0.120, 0.070, 0.040, 0.015, 0.004, 0.001]. The device power curve (DOE Reference Model 1) uses velocities (m/s) [0.0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 4.5, 5.0] and power (kW) [0, 0, 15, 52, 123, 240, 414, 550, 550, 550, 550]. The system includes 10 devices with a rated capacity of 550 kW each. Apply losses (%) for array spacing (5.0), resource overprediction (5.0), transmission (2.0), downtime (5.0), and additional factors (3.0). For the LCOE calculation, use a capital cost of $6,000/kW ($33,000,000 total), fixed O&M cost of $200/kW-yr ($1,100,000/yr total), variable O&M of $0/kWh, and a fixed charge rate of 10.8%. Save the Python script to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/tidal_array_model.py and the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/tidal_array_results.json. The JSON must contain exactly these keys: annual_energy_kwh, capacity_factor_percent, device_average_power_kw, number_devices, device_rated_capacity_kw, total_system_capacity_kw, lcoe_cents_per_kwh, total_capital_cost_usd, and annual_om_cost_usd.
